name: Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail
name_original: Ermita de la Virgen del Ara – circular desde Fuente del Arco
description: Hike a moderate 9.6-mile circular route from Fuente del Arco to the historic Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara, gaining 1404 feet.
region_intro: Hiking around Fuente Del Arco offers diverse landscapes, characterized by rolling hills, historical sites, and open countryside. The region features a mix of easy paths and more challenging trails, often leading through areas with cultural significance. Hikers can explore varied terrain, from gentle slopes to more elevated sections, providing different perspectives of the local environment. Best hiking trails around Fuente Del Arco The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail, a 9.6 miles (15.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route traverses the Sierra de Fuente del Arco, offering expansive views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail, an easy 3.0 miles (4.8 km) path. This trail explores historical sites, providing a blend of cultural interest and natural scenery. Local hikers also love the La Capitana Viewpoint loop from Guadalcanal, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain to a prominent viewpoint, often completed in about 3 hours 15 minutes. Hiking around Fuente Del Arco is defined by its sierras, historical landmarks, and scenic viewpoints. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate ascents. The routes in Fuente Del Arco are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from 11 reviews. More than 52 hikers have used komoot to explore Fuente Del Arco's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Fuente Del Arco?
A: There are &lt;strong&gt;16 hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt; around Fuente Del Arco, offering a variety of experiences. These routes range from easy strolls to more challenging moderate ascents, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners in Fuente Del Arco?
A: Yes, Fuente Del Arco offers &lt;strong&gt;4 easy hiking trails&lt;/strong&gt;. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991585002/alcazaba-de-reina-and-regina-turdulorum-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.0 miles (4.8 km) long and explores historical sites, blending cultural interest with natural scenery. Another easy choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Tendero loop from Fuente del Arco&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.7 km route.

Q: What are some family-friendly hiking options in Fuente Del Arco?
A: For families, the easier trails are generally best. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991585002/alcazaba-de-reina-and-regina-turdulorum-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a good choice due to its manageable length and historical points of interest, which can engage younger hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Tendero loop from Fuente del Arco&lt;/a&gt; is also an accessible option for a family outing.

Q: Are there any circular walks available in Fuente Del Arco?
A: Yes, several circular walks are available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Capitana Viewpoint loop from Guadalcanal&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 7.1 miles (11.4 km) circular route that leads to a prominent viewpoint. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850143" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro Tendero loop from Fuente del Arco&lt;/a&gt;, which is an easy 5.7 km circuit.

Q: What kind of historical sites or landmarks can I see while hiking in Fuente Del Arco?
A: Hiking in Fuente Del Arco often leads you through areas rich in history. You can explore sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5423343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman city of Regina Turdulorum&lt;/a&gt;, which is directly featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991585002/alcazaba-de-reina-and-regina-turdulorum-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcazaba de Reina and Regina Turdulorum Trail&lt;/a&gt;. Other notable landmarks include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5326622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5332635" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Views of Castillo de Reina&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The hiking routes in Fuente Del Arco are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of &lt;strong&gt;4.6 stars from 11 reviews&lt;/strong&gt;. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the blend of historical sites with natural scenery, and the expansive views offered, particularly along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991580069/sierra-de-fuente-del-arco-traverse-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any trails that offer good viewpoints in the region?
A: Yes, several trails provide excellent viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Capitana Viewpoint loop from Guadalcanal&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to lead to a prominent scenic outlook. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991580069/sierra-de-fuente-del-arco-traverse-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail&lt;/a&gt; offers expansive views across the sierra.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for hiking, with milder temperatures and pleasant conditions. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late afternoons are preferable if hiking during this season. Winters are generally mild, making hiking possible year-round, though some trails might be muddy after rain.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or parks near the hiking trails?
A: Yes, the region is close to significant natural areas. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1137424" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Aracena and Picos de Aroche Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument nearby, offering further opportunities for exploration. You can also find beautiful natural features like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5337978" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The holm oaks approaching Santa María de Nava&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest hiking trail in Fuente Del Arco?
A: The longest hiking trail in the area is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991580069/sierra-de-fuente-del-arco-traverse-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 9.6 miles (15.4 km) and typically takes about 4 hours 30 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers extensive views across the sierra.

Q: Are there any moderate difficulty trails for experienced hikers?
A: Yes, the majority of trails in Fuente Del Arco are of moderate difficulty, with &lt;strong&gt;12 such routes&lt;/strong&gt; available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e991580069/sierra-de-fuente-del-arco-traverse-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Fuente del Arco Traverse Trail&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31850509" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Capitana Viewpoint loop from Guadalcanal&lt;/a&gt; are excellent choices for those seeking a more challenging experience with varied terrain and ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Fuente Del Arco?
A: The terrain around Fuente Del Arco is diverse, characterized by rolling hills, open countryside, and sierras. You can expect a mix of gentle paths, more elevated sections, and varied surfaces, often leading through areas with cultural significance. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39552766" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Cerro San Miguel&lt;/a&gt; offer a good example of this varied landscape.
tips:
The Hermitage of the Virgen del Ara, located in Fuente del Arco, Badajoz, is known as the “Sistine Chapel of Extremadura” due to its impressive mural paintings. This Mudejar-style temple is decorated with frescoes depicting scenes from Genesis, attributed to the school of the painter Francisco de Zurbarán. 
The hermitage is located in a privileged natural environment, surrounded by olive trees and at the foot of the Sierra Morena. It is a place full of mysticism and beauty, ideal for a cultural and spiritual visit.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this hike?
faqs.answer: This moderate hike usually takes about 4 hours and 31 minutes to complete. This duration is for the full 15.4-kilometer loop.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied, characteristic of the Sierra Morena foothills. You'll encounter a mix of well-defined paths and potentially rougher, uneven sections, requiring good fitness and sure-footedness. Expect to traverse through olive groves and Mediterranean vegetation.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is the Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara, a 12th-century Mudejar-style building known as the "Sistine Chapel of Extremadura" for its impressive mural paintings. The trail also offers scenic views of olive groves and the natural environment surrounding the Sierra Morena mountains.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate, requiring a good fitness level and sure-footedness due to some uneven sections. While not ideal for absolute beginners, experienced hikers and active families might find it rewarding. Consider the 15.4 km distance and 430 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is this trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, always ensure your dog is under control, preferably on a leash, and be mindful of other hikers and any local wildlife. Remember to carry water for your pet, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are generally the best times to hike this trail. The weather is milder, and the natural scenery is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making the hike more challenging, while winters can bring cooler temperatures.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route starting from Fuente del Arco. You can typically find parking within the village, close to the main access points for the trail. Specific parking areas are usually well-marked or can be found near the town center.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike this trail. Access to the natural areas is generally free. There might be a small fee if you wish to enter the Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara itself, so it's advisable to check its opening hours and potential entry costs beforehand.

faqs.question: Is there water available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there is a water source located a few meters from the Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara. This can be useful for refilling water bottles. However, it's always recommended to carry sufficient water for the entire hike, especially on warmer days.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Camino de la Serrezuela, Cañada Real del Pencón, and Calle Valentín Murillo, among others. These are official trails that share parts of the same path.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect on this hike?
faqs.answer: You can expect a privileged natural environment with extensive olive groves and the rolling hills of the Sierra Morena foothills. The scenery is typical Mediterranean, offering a blend of agricultural landscapes and natural beauty, culminating in the cultural landmark of the hermitage.

waypoints.name: Hermitage of the Virgin of Ara
